records.ety: [OE. swain, swein, Icel. sveinn a boy, servant; akin to Sw. sven, Dan. svend, AS. swān, OHG. swein.]

quotes: Him behoves serve himself that has no swain.

defs: A young man dwelling in the country; a rustic; esp., a cuntry gallant or lover; -- chiefly in poetry.
quotes: It were a happy life To be no better than a homely swain.
Blest swains! whose nymphs in every grace excel.
